sn22.16:1.1: At Sāvatthī. "Bhikkhus, form is suffering. What is suffering is nonself. What is nonself should be seen as it really is with correct wisdom thus: ‘This is not mine, this I am not, this is not my self.’
sn22.16:1.2: "Feeling is suffering … . Perception is suffering … . Volitional formations are suffering … . Consciousness is suffering. What is suffering is nonself. What is nonself should be seen as it really is with correct wisdom thus: ‘This is not mine, this I am not, this is not my self.’
sn22.16:1.3: "Seeing thus … He understands: ‘ … there is no more for this state of being.’"
